display.purpose: Hero headings only. Space Grotesk 600 at large clamp scales.

title.purpose: Card titles, form step names, template labels.

body.purpose: Prose, AI-generated content, form descriptions. Inter for max legibility.

label.purpose: All structural chrome: stage labels, field names, coordinates. JetBrains Mono uppercase.

shadows.name: ambient-deep
shadows.purpose: Portfolio frame only — the output document sitting on the workbench. One per page.

shadows.name: hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary button hover state. Chromatic lift, not a structural shadow.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Active stage dots, interactive focus states.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: All state transitions — fast out, no bounce. System default.

motion.name: duration-mid
motion.purpose: Theme toggle knob, stage dot state changes.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Single column layout; stage labels hide; grid switches to 1-col.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main CTA. Amber fill, navy text, 3px radius. Never rounded beyond this.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 14px; padding: 13px 26px; border-radius: 3px; border: 1.5px solid #F2A65A; background: #F2A65A; color: #0A1628; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(.22,.9,.31,1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(.22,.9,.31,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(242,166,90,0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(242,166,90,0.25); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary CTA. Transparent bg, shifts border+text to amber on hover. Never fills on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 14px; padding: 13px 26px; border-radius: 3px; border: 1.5px solid #2E5580; background: transparent; color: #EAF2FB; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #F2A65A; color: #F2A65A; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(242,166,90,0.25); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field. Elevated navy surface, 1px border, shifts to amber on focus.
components.css: .ds-field-group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #8CA3C2; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #0F2137; border: 1px solid #1E3A5F; border-radius: 3px; padding: 12px 14px; color: #EAF2FB; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #F2A65A; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8CA3C2; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.description: Skill/tech tag pill. 14px radius — pill exception to 3px system rule.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #142B45; border: 1px solid #2E5580; padding: 5px 10px; border-radius: 14px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; color: #EAF2FB; } .ds-chip-rm { background: none; border: none; color: #8CA3C2; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1; cursor: pointer; padding: 0; } .ds-chip-rm:hover { color: #F2735A; }

components.name: Elevated Card
components.description: Standard elevated card surface. Tonal lift from bg, 1px border, 6px radius. No shadow at rest.
components.html:
01

Fill in your details

Name, role, skills, and projects. 5 steps, 5 minutes.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0F2137; border: 1px solid #1E3A5F; border-radius: 6px; padding: 22px 24px; position: relative; } .ds-card-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; color: #8CA3C2; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; color: #EAF2FB; margin: 8px 0 6px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; color: #8CA3C2; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Mono Eyebrow
components.description: Section eyebrow: mono label with amber left-rule. Used deliberately — not on every section.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #F2A65A; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-eyebrow::before { content: ''; width: 16px; height: 1px; background: #F2A65A; }

components.name: Stage Dot
components.description: Progress indicator dot. Three states: active (amber + ring), done (teal), upcoming (border-strong).
components.css: .ds-stage { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; border: 1.5px solid #2E5580; background: transparent; transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(.22,.9,.31,1); } .ds-dot--done { background: #5EEAD4; border-color: #5EEAD4; } .ds-dot--active { background: #F2A65A; border-color: #F2A65A; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(242,166,90,0.25); } .ds-line { width: 26px; height: 1px; background: #2E5580; }

narrative.overview:
BLUEPRINT's visual system is what a precision drafting table would look like as software: dark, structured, technical — but inhabited. The navy-deep background reads like drafting paper in a darkened studio; the amber accent is the glow of the desk lamp falling across a schematic. Every element earns its place because drafting tools are built for focus, not decoration.

The system is committed to contrast as craft. The dark-mode base (#0A1628) is not 'dark because tools look cool dark' — it's because junior developers are already living in dark terminals, dark editors, dark IDEs. BLUEPRINT speaks their ambient language.

This system explicitly rejects: generic SaaS portfolio builders, enterprise overbuilt tools, and the visual grammar of AI-slop generators — cream backgrounds, gradient text, glassmorphism-as-default, identical card grids, eyebrow labels on every section.
keyCharacteristics: Dark navy base with amber accent; teal as secondary signal color (completion, success)
Monospace type (JetBrains Mono) for all labels, metadata, and structural chrome
Sharp corners (border-radius: 3px) as the system default; curves only for pills and circles
Grid backdrop and crop-mark decoration — structural, not decorative
Motion: cubic-bezier(.22,.9,.31,1) — fast out, no bounce; state changes only at rest

rules.name: The Amber Rarity Rule
rules.body: Amber (#F2A65A) appears on ≤15% of any given screen. CTAs, active states, focus outlines, eyebrow lines. When everything is amber, nothing is amber.

rules.name: The Muted Gray Ceiling
rules.body: Muted text (#8CA3C2) is for metadata labels and supporting copy only. Never use it for body prose or any text the user needs to read to complete a task.

rules.name: The Mono-as-Chrome Rule
rules.body: JetBrains Mono is reserved for the interface talking about itself — stage labels, field names, coordinate metadata, machine states. Running body copy is always Inter.

rules.name: The Display Ceiling Rule
rules.body: No heading exceeds clamp(38px, 6vw, 68px). Letter-spacing floor at -0.02em for display. Anything tighter than -0.04em makes letters touch and reads as cramped.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Cards, panels, and elevated surfaces use tonal color shifts (--bg-elev) to indicate layering. Shadows appear only when an element genuinely floats above the page in the user's mental model.

rules.name: The No-Nested-Cards Rule
rules.body: Cards do not contain cards. When a card surface needs to hold sub-items, use dashed borders or divider lines.

rules.name: The Grid-As-Context Rule
rules.body: The blueprint grid and crop marks appear only on the app shell (the drafting table). They must be absent from the generated portfolio frame.

rules.name: The Ghost Transition Rule
rules.body: Ghost buttons only shift border and text color to amber on hover — never fill the background. A ghost button that fills has become a primary button mid-interaction, which is incoherent.

rules.name: The Two-Surface Rule
rules.body: BLUEPRINT has two architecturally separate design surfaces: the app shell (tokens: --bg, --ink, --text, --border, --muted) and the output frame (.portfolio-frame, tokens: --p-bg, --p-surface, --p-text, --p-muted, --p-accent, --p-font). A rule in .portfolio-frame or any .tmpl-* selector is output-layer code. Never use a skin rule as evidence that a pattern is acceptable in the app shell.

dos: Do use JetBrains Mono for all structural labels, field names, stage indicators, and machine-state text.
Do keep amber (#F2A65A) rare — CTAs, active states, eyebrow lines.
Do use 3px radius as the default for all interactive controls.
Do use tonal elevation (color shift from --bg to --bg-elev to --bg-elev-2) rather than shadows to show depth.
Do keep the blueprint grid and crop-mark decoration only on the app shell.
Do treat .tmpl-* CSS as output-layer code — if a skin rule appears to violate a DESIGN.md rule, check the selector first; if it's scoped to .tmpl-*, it's a documented exception, not a bug.
Do keep .portfolio-frame as a single-root DOM element that is never an ancestor of app shell chrome.
Do verify that muted blue text (#8CA3C2) is only used for metadata labels, not body prose.
Do use text-wrap: balance on display headings to prevent orphaned words.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with linear-gradient). Never.

Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur + translucent fill) on cards, list items, or callouts in the app shell. The .tmpl-glass skin uses it legitimately inside .portfolio-frame — that is a documented exception, not a precedent.
Don't add new .tmpl-* selectors that apply outside .portfolio-frame. The scope guard depends on this containment.
Don't use border-left or border-right wider than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items.
Don't use border-radius greater than 16px on cards or section containers.
Don't pair border: 1px solid X with box-shadow blur ≥ 16px on the same element (the ghost-card pattern).
Don't use cream, sand, beige, or warm-neutral body backgrounds as defaults.
Don't apply eyebrow labels to every section — one deliberate eyebrow per surface, not a scaffold on every heading.
Don't design the app shell to look like a generic SaaS product builder (Wix, Squarespace, Canva).
Don't use sketchy SVG illustrations or feTurbulence paper-grain filters.
Don't use repeating-linear-gradient stripe backgrounds or decorative two-axis CSS grid overlays.
